zh.lifeSummary: 當Catherine Alice Baldwin於1456年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓時，她的父親Sir John Richard Baldwin41歲，母親Agnes Augustina Dormer32歲。她和Thomas Apuke至少有1個兒子。她去世於1561年4月20日在她的家鄉享年105歲，埋葬在英國巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓聖 邁克爾 安德 哥吉拉 安爾也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Baldwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’, which was extremely popular among the Normans and in Flanders in the early Middle Ages. It was the personal name of the Crusader who in 1100 became the first Christian king of Jerusalem, and of four more Crusader kings of Jerusalem. It was also borne by Baldwin, Count of Flanders (1172–1205), leader of the Fourth Crusade, who became first Latin Emperor of Constantinople (1204). In North America, this surname has absorbed Dutch forms such as Boudewijn.
Irish: surname adopted in Donegal by bearers of the Gaelic surname Ó Maolagáin (see Milligan ), due to association of Gaelic maol ‘bald, hairless’ with English bald.
History: A John Baldwin from Buckinghamshire, England, arrived in the US in 1638 and settled in Milford, CT.

variants.name: Milligan
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolagáin ‘descendant of Maoileagán’, a personal name from a double diminutive of maol meaning ‘bald’ or ‘tonsured’.

variants.name: Baldovino
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Philippines and Colombia) and Italian: from the personal name Baldovino, from ancient Germanic Baldowin (see Baldwin ).

variants.name: Bellmann
meanings: North German:
habitational name for someone from Belle in Westphalia, Bell in the Rhineland, or Bellen near Bremen.
in Saxony, from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal name   Baldwin  .

variants.name: Bais
meanings: Dutch (mainly North Holland): patronymic from the personal name Baije, which is a derivative of an ancient Germanic name beginning with the element bern- ‘bear’, or possibly a variant of Boije, which may be a derivative of Boidin and thus a variant of Baldwin .
Italian (Friuli Venezia Giulia and Trentino-South Tyrol): nickname derived from German weiss ‘white’.

Americanized form of Croatian and Slovenian Bajs: nickname from dialect bajs ‘bass’ (the musical instrument), figuratively ‘fatty, potbelly’. The surname in the form Bais is found in Croatia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Baudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Etienne, Adrien, Alcide, Andre, Firmin, Francoise, Jean-Yves, Laurent, Minos, Sylvain.
French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Boudoin 1 and Bowdoin .
Altered form of French   Beaudoin  , a cognate (variant) of 1 above.

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Balkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Herschel.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish female personal name Beyle meaning ‘beautiful’ (related to French belle).

English (of Norman origin): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Baldekin, Baudekin, an Anglo-Norman borrowing of Middle Dutch Baudequin, a Flemish pet form of Baldwin . However, a clear link between Middle English Baudekin and post-medieval Balkin has not been established, and the modern name may have a different source, a variant of Balcom perhaps, by migration.

variants.name: Bawden
meanings: English: from a late variant of the Norman personal name   Baldwin   or a variant of   Bowden  .

variants.name: Baldon
meanings: English: habitational name from either Marsh Baldon or Toot Baldon in Oxfordshire, from the Old English personal name Bealda + dūn ‘hill’, or a variant of Baldwin . Occasionally the name may be from Baildon in Yorkshire (named with Old English bēgel ‘bend’ + dūn ‘hill’; recorded as Baldon in the 16th century), or from Ballidon in Derbyshire (named with Old English belg ‘bag, sack’ + denu ‘valley’).
English: variant of   Boldon  .
Italian (Veneto): from an augmentative of the personal name   Baldo  .

variants.name: Beaudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Serge, Jacques, Gilles, Laurent, Normand, Pierre, Alcide, Lucien, Raoul.

French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Baudoin , Baudouin , Beaudion , Boadway , Boardway , Bodeau , Bodoh , Bodway , Boudway , and Budway .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Catherine Alice Baldwin was born in 1456, in Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Sir John Richard Baldwin, was 41 and her mother, Agnes Augustina Dormer, was 32. She had at least 1 son with Thomas Apuke. She died on 20 April 1561, in her hometown, at the age of 105, and was buried in St Michael and All Angels' Church, Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Catherine Alice Baldwin(이)가 1456년 영국 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Richard Baldwin(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Augustina Dormer(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 Thomas Apuke(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1561년 4월 20일 그녀의 고향에서 105세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴 스트 믹하얼 앤드 앨 앤그스' c후르치에 묻혔다.

У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Thomas Apuke. Она умерла 20 апреля 1561 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 105 лет и была похоронена в местности ст мачаел анд алл ангелс' чурч, астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия.
